title: Assistance for American Families and Workers
summary: This page describes four COVID-19 related fiscal assistance programs administered by the Treasury Department: Economic Impact Payments (direct relief sent in three rounds), Unemployment Compensation (extended via American Rescue Plan), an expanded Child Tax Credit, and Emergency Rental Assistance. The content is informational and describes historical programs from 2020-2021 rather than providing current market-relevant analysis or policy announcements.
key_points: Treasury Department, Bureau of Fiscal Service, and IRS distributed three rounds of Economic Impact Payments during COVID-19 crisis
American Rescue Plan (March 2021) extended unemployment compensation and waived some federal taxes on unemployment benefits
American Rescue Plan increased Child Tax Credit and expanded coverage to assist families with children
Emergency Rental Assistance program makes funding available to government entities to help households unable to pay rent or utilities
why_it_matters: This source provides no current market-relevant information. It describes historical COVID-era fiscal stimulus programs that have already concluded. There is no discussion of macro implications, market impact, or policy path forward. Not useful for real-time or forward-looking financial analysis.
